content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


April 3, 2001


Fazle Rab Quadri
District Counsel
Mojave Desert Air Quality Management District
14306 Park Avenue
Victorville, CA 92392

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-01-066

Dear Mr. Quadri:

This is in response to your request for information on behalf of District Board Member Bill Postmus, who is also an elected member of the San Bernardino County Board of Supervisors, concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You have asked whether the $100 threshold for itemizing contributions received, as established in section 84211(j), has changed. Although several legislative amendments to the Act's reporting provisions went into effect on January 1, 2001, there has been no change in the $100 threshold for itemizing contributions. For your information, enclosed is a copy of the Commission's November 2000 FPPC Bulletin, which contains a summary of the legislative changes (see pages 17-18).

In addition, Proposition 34 was approved by the voters in the November 2000 General Election and made significant changes to the Act's campaign provisions. Although most of the changes enacted in Proposition 34 affect candidates for elective state office and state committees, several provisions are applicable to local candidates and officeholders as well. A chart explaining these provisions is also enclosed.


¹ Gov. Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

Fazle Rab Quadri
Our File No. G-01-066
Page 2

If you need additional assistance, please contact me at our toll-free number (866)
275-3772.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the $100 threshold for itemizing campaign contributions has changed under the Political Reform Act, and what the impact of recent legislative amendments and Proposition 34 are on campaign disclosure provisions for local officials.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The $100 threshold for itemizing contributions remains unchanged despite legislative amendments effective January 1, 2001 and the passage of Proposition 34 in November 2000. While Proposition 34 made significant changes to state campaign provisions, several provisions also apply to local candidates and officeholders.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the $100 threshold for itemizing campaign contributions has changed under the Political Reform Act, and what the impact of recent legislative amendments and Proposition 34 are on campaign disclosure provisions for local officials.

CONCLUSION: The $100 threshold for itemizing contributions remains unchanged despite legislative amendments effective January 1, 2001 and the passage of Proposition 34 in November 2000. While Proposition 34 made significant changes to state campaign provisions, several provisions also apply to local candidates and officeholders.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that the $100 contribution itemization threshold under Political Reform Act Section 84211(f) has not changed, and providing information about January 2001 legislative amendments and Proposition 34's impact on campaign disclosure requirements.
